title: Converting one Inverse T function to other Inverse T

concepts.title: Angle sum properties Part 1
concepts.conceptBody:
Angle sum properties

(1) If $x>0,y>0$ then

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}y & ~=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x+y}{1-xy} \right) & {} & \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ if }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }xy<1 \\ {} & ~=\pi +\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x+y}{1-xy} \right) & {} & \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ if }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }xy>1 \\ \end{array}$

 

(2) If $x>0,y>0$ then

$\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x-\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}y=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x-y}{1+xy} \right)$

(3) If $x>0$

$\left. \begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x=\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\sqrt{1-{{x}^{2}}},\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}x=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\sqrt{1-{{x}^{2}}} \\ \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-{{x}^{2}}}},\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}x=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{\sqrt{1-{{x}^{2}}}}{2} \\ \end{array} \right\}-1<x<1$

examples.title: The value of $\sin ^{-\cot ^{-1}} \tan ^{-\cos ^{-1} x}$ is equal to
If $x$ takes non-positive permissible value, then $\sin ^{-1} x=$
The value of $x$ which satisfies the equation $\tan ^{-1} x=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}\right)$ is
$\sin ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{5}$ for some $x \in(-1,1)$, then the value of $\cos ^{-1} x$ is
